title: carum vaginatum
intro: Carum vaginatum, also known as Ajwain, is a small annual herb native to the Middle East and the Indian subcontinent, characterized by its small, greenish-yellow flowers and distinctive seed pods. The medicinal properties of Ajwain lie in its essential oils, which have antimicrobial and carminative effects, making it a popular remedy for digestive issues such as indigestion, bloating, and gas. The health benefits of Ajwain include relief from respiratory problems like bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of Ajwain include teas, infusions, and powders, which can be used internally to aid digestion or externally as a topical treatment for skin conditions.
